title: ZRC-ND-3.5M Guard validation package
objective: Determine whether the ZRC-ND-3.5M Guard lead is suitable to enter biological conditioned-medium follow-up by proving small-waste exchange, trophic-factor retention, low material interference, and stable module behavior in non-cell media tests.

scope.claim_after_success: Ready for conditioned-medium and biological follow-up, not yet proven safe for direct or long-duration neural culture use.
scope.lead_design: 3.5 kDa regenerated-cellulose dialysis membrane with MPC-like zwitterionic wetted-surface modification, low-binding PES/SFCA guard prefilter, and COC/COP monitored housing.
scope.design_boundary: Side-stream cartridge outside the cell chamber; no direct cell exposure before blank and recovery gates pass.
scope.not_in_scope: Final sterilization validation, GMP manufacturing, implantable/device claims, and CL1 API integration.

procurement_specs.required_spec: Regenerated-cellulose dialysis membrane or cassette, nominal 3.5 kDa MWCO, low nonspecific protein adsorption, aqueous medium compatibility, enough area to fabricate replicate coupons or small side-stream modules.
procurement_specs.preferred_format: Closed cassette or flat/tubular membrane coupon that can be placed in an external loop without exposing cells to adhesives or unvalidated housing.
procurement_specs.reject_if: Cellulose ester only without regenerated-cellulose comparator, unknown MWCO, high protein binding, incompatible preservatives, or no usable extractables/handling information.

procurement_specs.required_spec: Regenerated-cellulose dialysis membrane or cassette, nominal 10 kDa MWCO, same geometry class as the 3.5 kDa lead when possible.
procurement_specs.preferred_format: Matched coupon or cassette for retention-clearance boundary testing.
procurement_specs.reject_if: Geometry, area, or handling differs enough that clearance cannot be compared to the 3.5 kDa lead.

procurement_specs.required_spec: Sterile low-protein-binding PES or SFCA filter, preferably 0.22 um for debris/sterility-style guarding in a short upstream position.
procurement_specs.preferred_format: Small inline or syringe-filter format that can be bypassed for matched no-guard controls.
procurement_specs.reject_if: High protein binding, surfactant leaching risk, unknown membrane material, or large hold-up volume that changes medium composition.

procurement_specs.required_spec: COC/COP or PEEK wetted housing with low extractables risk, optical observation of bubbles/precipitate, and ports for pH, conductivity, and flow/pressure monitoring.
procurement_specs.preferred_format: COC/COP first, PEEK backup; avoid PDMS in the lead path unless adsorption is intentionally being tested.
procurement_specs.reject_if: PDMS as primary wetted housing, unknown adhesive exposure, opaque housing that prevents bubble checks, or no blank-extractables plan.

procurement_specs.required_spec: Primary coupon route: PDA/polyMPC controlled surface-only deposition with pore-protection/backflow or equivalent pore-preserving process control. Backup routes: PMMMSi MPC-silane crosslinked brush or PSBMA cellulose brush only after the primary route fails a defined gate.
procurement_specs.preferred_format: Thin, stable surface treatment with matched unmodified RC and housing controls, measured before and after coating for MWCO/transport shift.
procurement_specs.reject_if: Coating blocks low-MW transport, changes MWCO unpredictably, sheds measurable extractables, or lowers BDNF/NGF recovery.

test_articles.description: 3.5 kDa RC exchange element, MPC-like zwitterionic wetted-surface modification, low-binding PES/SFCA guard, COC/COP housing.
3.5 kDa RC exchange element with the same guard and housing but no zwitterionic modification.
10 kDa RC exchange element with the same zwitterionic/guard/housing concept.
Matched medium held without cartridge exposure.
Conventional medium refresh reference for waste dilution and secretome dilution comparison.

run_matrix.name: Fresh-medium material blank
run_matrix.purpose: Detect pH, osmolality, conductivity, turbidity, and extractables shifts before any biological material is used.
inputs: fresh complete neuronal medium or closest CL1-like medium proxy

run_matrix.name: Spiked recovery and clearance panel
run_matrix.purpose: Measure whether the module removes lactate/ammonium-class wastes while retaining trophic proteins and bulk proteins.

run_matrix.name: Conditioned-medium non-cell challenge
run_matrix.purpose: Stress the cartridge with protein-rich or aged neural-conditioned medium without exposing live cells to unvalidated materials.
inputs: conditioned medium or aged medium proxy
timepoints: repeat exposure if fouling is low

assay_panel.control_requirement: record pH; run standards in matched medium

assay_panel.sample_matrix: spiked medium and conditioned-medium proxy when available
assay_panel.control_requirement: matrix spike/recovery and no-cartridge stability control

assay_panel.sample_matrix: spiked medium and conditioned-medium proxy when available
assay_panel.control_requirement: matrix spike/recovery and no-cartridge stability control

assay_panel.readout: Albumin, transferrin, and total protein recovery
assay_panel.method_class: protein-specific assay plus total protein assay
assay_panel.control_requirement: no-cartridge stability and unmodified RC baseline

assay_panel.readout: pH, osmolality, conductivity, turbidity, and visible precipitate
assay_panel.method_class: standard media chemistry and visual inspection
assay_panel.sample_matrix: fresh medium blank and all recovery samples
assay_panel.control_requirement: same timepoints for no-module static control

assay_panel.readout: Flow resistance, bubble events, and apparent fouling/flux decline
assay_panel.method_class: inline flow/pressure proxy and observation log
assay_panel.control_requirement: record module geometry, membrane area, flow rate, and exposure time for normalization

acceptance_gates.criterion: Fresh-medium blank shows no material-driven pH drift greater than 0.10 pH units, no osmolality or conductivity drift greater than 5 percent versus no-module control, no visible precipitate/turbidity increase, and no unresolved extractables concern.
acceptance_gates.rationale: No biological follow-up should begin if the cartridge changes base medium chemistry.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Change housing, guard, coating, rinse/preconditioning, or membrane source before retesting.

acceptance_gates.criterion: BDNF/NGF or validated proxy recovery is at least 90 percent versus no-module control, with albumin/transferrin/total protein recovery also at least 90 percent.
acceptance_gates.rationale: The lead is selected to preserve neural signaling context rather than maximize waste clearance.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Lower MWCO, remove or change guard/coating, or reduce adsorption-prone wetted area.

acceptance_gates.criterion: Lactate and total ammonia/ammonium decrease at least 25 percent more than no-module control over the chosen exposure window without violating retention or blank-integrity gates.
acceptance_gates.rationale: The module must show useful waste exchange, not just passive compatibility.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Increase membrane area/exposure, consider 10 kDa challenge data, or add a second-stage confined polishing module.

acceptance_gates.criterion: Flow resistance or pressure proxy changes less than 20 percent during the test window, and clearance/retention does not degrade across repeat exposure.
acceptance_gates.rationale: A wetware life-support cartridge must fail visibly and slowly, not silently strip or clog.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Improve prefilter, surface modification, geometry, or cleaning/preconditioning strategy.

acceptance_gates.criterion: The lead must outperform unmodified 3.5 kDa RC on fouling or protein recovery while remaining safer than the 10 kDa challenge on retention.
acceptance_gates.rationale: This gate tests whether the zwitterionic lead adds value beyond a simple RC baseline.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: If unmodified RC performs equally well, downselect to simpler RC-3.5K Guard; if 10 kDa retains factors adequately, rerank the variants.

decision_rules.condition: All acceptance gates pass for ZRC-ND-3.5M Guard
decision_rules.action: Promote the technology from nominated to validation-passed-for-non-cell-media and design Phase D biological conditioned-medium pilot.

decision_rules.condition: Blank integrity passes, retention passes, but waste clearance fails
decision_rules.action: Keep 3.5 kDa as retention-safe baseline and optimize membrane area, exposure time, or a confined second-stage polishing module.

decision_rules.condition: Blank integrity passes, clearance passes, but factor retention fails
decision_rules.action: Reject the lead geometry for CL1-like neural medium and move to lower MWCO or lower-adsorption wetted surfaces.

decision_rules.condition: Zwitterionic lead does not beat unmodified 3.5 kDa RC
decision_rules.action: Demote the coating to optional; simplify toward RC-3.5K Guard until fouling data justify added chemistry.

decision_rules.condition: 10 kDa challenge passes retention and improves clearance
decision_rules.action: Rerun variant screen with measured retention/clearance values and reconsider 10 kDa as lead.
Do not expose cells; replace the suspect material or preconditioning workflow and repeat Phase A.
